news.t: Materials ETFs jump the most in 2 months
Materials ETFs see biggest gain in five weeks on hopes for economy
Basic Materials ETFs Poised for a September Bounce
How The Pieces Add Up: The Vanguard Materials ETF Targets $138

holdings.infoBox: VAW is an equity ETF with a total of 116 individual holdings. The top holdings are Linde stock at 14.78%, Newmont at 7.60%, Freeport-McMoRan at 6.05%, CRH plc at 4.67%, and The Sherwin-Williams Company at 4.58%.

overview.description: The Vanguard Materials ETF (VAW) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the MSCI US IMI 25/50 Materials index. The fund tracks a market cap-weighted index of companies in the US materials sector. VAW was launched on Jan 26, 2004 and is issued by Vanguard.

data.title: Materials ETFs jump the most in 2 months
data.text: Materials-sector exchange-traded funds jumped Monday as investors appeared to bet on sectors that stand to gain more as economic growth throttles higher. The Materials Select Sector SPDR Fund rose 2.5...

data.title: Materials ETFs see biggest gain in five weeks on hopes for economy
data.text: The materials Sector SPDR ETF rose 2% midday Monday, the strongest performer among the 11 funds tracking sectors of the S&P 500 . It was the fund's biggest gain in five weeks.

data.title: Basic Materials ETFs Poised for a September Bounce
data.text: Underperforming materials stocks may receive a boost from supportive policy action in September. Trade the sector using these ETFs.

data.title: How The Pieces Add Up: The Vanguard Materials ETF Targets $138
data.text: For the Vanguard Materials ETF, we found that the implied analyst target price for the ETF based upon its underlying holdings is $137.61 per unit.
